D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

Tone-Mapping Operators

a scene carries far more range than a screen — an operator decides how to fold the highlights down without crushing or clipping

A rendered scene holds luminances well beyond 1.0; a display tops out. A tone-mapping operator compresses that range into 0–1. Simple Reinhard never quite reaches white; the filmic Hable and the ACES fit add an S-shaped toe and shoulder that hold shadow and highlight detail the way film stock does.

Switch operators and raise exposure to push more scene into the highlights. Watch where each curve rolls off: hard clipping blows out, Reinhard desaturates everything, the filmic curves keep a graceful shoulder. The strip below previews a ramp through the chosen operator.
